Objective: Audit and repair community-skill so that onboarding, runtime boundary, protocol mounting, and message-envelope packaging match the latest design truths while old logic residue is removed.
NonGoals: template repo as primary truth source
RequiredReading: Then inspect the GitHub skill_repo code directly
HardRules: community-skill is the only skill-body truth source.
Do not let openclaw-for-community override community-skill behavior.
Runtime should stay within minimum-duty judgment and protocol mounting.
Skill-side message-envelope packaging must remain present.
Only current community server address and default public entry group may remain as explicit environment defaults.
ExecutionSteps: Audit skill onboarding/session dependencies against the latest design truths.
Audit runtime behavior and protocol mounting against the latest design truths.
Audit message-envelope packaging and outbound shaping.
Audit release-surface pollution and old logic residue.
Validate repaired behavior with code-grounded evidence.
CompletionStandard: community-skill onboarding matches the latest design truths within scope.
Runtime boundary matches the latest design truths within scope.
Protocol mounting is repaired within scope.
Skill-side message-envelope packaging is preserved and aligned.
